chief_complaint: A 75-year-old right-handed male presented with limited movement in his left limbs and difficulties with speech.
history: The patient has experienced a bilateral tremor in the upper limbs for 30 years, with exacerbation on the left side. He was hospitalized on January 1, 2021, due to his symptoms.
exam: Vital signs: not mentioned. Physical examination findings: The left finger-nose test lacked stability and precision.
labs: Cranial computed tomography revealed scattered lacunar infarctions in the brain. Magnetic resonance imaging revealed many lacunar lesions in the bilateral basal ganglia and the centrum semiovale. A fresh lesion was seen in the right centrum semiovale.
correct_diagnosis: Acute ischemic stroke with bilateral essential tremor improvement
differentials: Acute ischemic stroke with bilateral essential tremor improvement
correct_ranking: Acute ischemic stroke with bilateral essential tremor improvement
